name: Chioggia
image_alt: Sliced beet showing concentric pink and white rings
description: An Italian heirloom beet with concentric rings of pink and white inside — visually one of the most striking vegetables in the garden. Mild, sweet flavor with less of the earthy quality of dark beets.
history: Named for the fishing village of Chioggia near Venice, where it has been grown for centuries. Also called 'Bulls-eye beet' or 'Candy Cane beet' in American seed catalogs for its circus-like interior pattern.

bad.reason: Beans and beets inhibit each other

seasonal_quirks: Cool-season crop — best spring and fall. Heat causes zoning (the rings) to become less distinct and flavor to become earthier. The pink-white color fades to uniform pale pink when cooked.
harvest_cues: Best at 2–3 inches diameter. Larger roots become woodier and rings less distinct. Greens are edible — harvest outer leaves throughout the season.
variety_notes: Slice raw for salads to show the striking pattern — it disappears when cooked. The mild flavor makes it accessible to beet skeptics. Greens are among the most tender and mild of beet tops.
preservation: Roast and freeze. Pickle in spiced vinegar brine — color will fade but flavor is excellent.
